violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine taken out of service, ice disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2025

Pork belly, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per PIC interview of staff, pork belly was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2025

Working containers of multiple dry and liquid ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 05/26/2025

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2025

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. PIC relocated ice scoop. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -vent guards of the ice machine -bottom shelving of refrigeration units. New Violation. Correct By: 05/26/2025
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2025
Leaf litter and debris was observed around the dumpster area. Dumpster areas must be maintained clean to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 05/26/2025

The door to the outside, located in the rear, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024
Dispenser for quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution was not dispensing at correct concentration at 3 bay sink. PIC informed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3 bay. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024
The ceiling in the basement area is in poor repair. Physical facilities to be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2024
Excessive leaves observed under patio deck. Establishments shall be kept free of litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/15/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. State guidance provided to establishment. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of pink and black debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service during time of inspection. Ice was voluntarily disposed of. Ice machine cannot be used until equipment is properly cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024

Miso soup was observed held at (77) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. soup was reheated to 200 degrees Fahrenheit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024

Quaternary ammonium sanitizer solution was in excess of (400) parts per million (ppm). A quaternary ammonia sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be applied at the strength as indicated on the manufacturers' label. readjusted during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024

The presence of large rodents is not controlled as evidenced by burrow holes and rodent carcasses observed by the dumpster area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2024

The door to the outside, located in the rear, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2024
Utensils (chopsticks and rice scoop) were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024
Single service items in the basement were observed stored on the floor in the basement. Single service items to be at least 6 inches off the ground. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024
Dispenser for quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution was not dispensing at correct concentration. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2024

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the (chlorine and quaternary ammonium) sanitizing solutions for the (dishmachine and 3 bay sinks). New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2024

The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. Litter was also observed by refuse units. Area to be cleaned and free of trash and litter to prevent harborage conditions for pests. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2024
The ceiling in the basement area is in poor repair. Several holes were observed on walls in basement. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2024
Excessive leaves observed under patio deck. Establishments shall be kept free litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2024
